name: Sesame Seed Puffs (Jeen Duey)
recipeIngredient: 1 recipe of the sweet glutinous rice dough
recipeInstructions:
--------------------------
FILLING:
1. Divide the dough in half and separate each half into 12 portions.
2. Roll each portion to form a ball.
3. Flatten by hand to a 3-inch round.
4. Put 1 teaspoon of red bean paste or sweet crescent filling in the center.
5. Press opposite sides together to seal.
6. Roll again in palms to form a round ball.
7. Roll the ball in sesame seeds.
COOKING:
8. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat (350 degrees in a deep fryer).
9. Deep fry four balls per batch, moving them constantly so they do not stay at the bottom.
10. After 2-3 minutes, the balls will start to float.
11. Rotate the balls with chopsticks or a slotted spoon, gently pushing them against the side of the wok (this action will help the balls retain a rounder shape).
12. Continue frying until the balls attain a medium golden brown color (approximately 8-10 minutes).
13. Drain on paper towel and cool for 5 minutes before serving.
DO AHEAD NOTES:
Jein Dueys can be made ahead and kept refrigerated for several days. Reheat by deep frying or in an oven at 350 degrees for 10 minutes.
